rxn_class_summary: Nickel-catalyzed electrochemical reductive cyclizative carboxylation of alkene-tethered carbamoyl chlorides with atmospheric CO2 to give 2-oxoindolin-3-ylacetic acids and 2-oxotetrahydroquinoline-4-carboxylic acids
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of NiCl2(bpp) to Ni(0), which performs oxidative addition into the C(acyl)-Cl bond; further single-electron cathodic reductions turn over the Ni catalyst across the cycle. Mg sacrificial anode balances charge.

key_intermediates.name: Ni(II)-acyl chloride intermediate A from oxidative addition of carbamoyl chloride 1a
Ni(I)-acyl intermediate B (from SET reduction of A, path a)
Ni(II) cyclized alkyl intermediates C/C' (intramolecular alkene migratory insertion, path b)
Ni(I) cyclized alkyl intermediates D/D' after SET reduction
Ni-carboxylate intermediates E/E' after CO2 insertion into Ni-C bond

electron_flow: Cathode reduces NiCl2(bpp) to Ni(0); Ni(0) does oxidative addition into the carbamoyl C-Cl bond of 1 to give Ni(II) acyl A. A is reduced at the cathode (path a: SET first to Ni(I) B, then alkene cyclization to D/D'; path b: alkene cyclization first to Ni(II) C/C', then SET to D/D'). CO2 inserts into the Ni-C bond to give carboxylate E/E'; a final cathodic reduction releases Ni(0) and, after aqueous workup, the carboxylic acid product. Mg anode is sacrificially oxidized.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of NiCl2(bpp), 1a, and CO2
probe_reagents.observation: NiCl2(bpp) shows two reversible reductive peaks at -1.71 V and -2.31 V (Ni(II)->Ni(I)->Ni(0)); adding 1a produces strong catalytic current near -2.50 V indicating oxidative addition with in situ Ni(0); saturating with CO2 further increases the reductive current, consistent with carboxylation turnover.

notes: Two cyclization pathways are proposed and considered competitive: path a (SET reduction of Ni(II) acyl A to Ni(I) B before alkene migratory insertion) and path b (alkene insertion first to give Ni(II) C/C', then SET to D/D'). The paper does not designate one as strictly favored; both converge on D/D' prior to CO2 insertion. Substrate scope shows ortho/meta/para electronic effects and poor performance with aryl chloride/bromide substituents on the arene (competing reactivity), and 1n (bromo on indoline-forming arene) gives no carboxylation product, consistent with competing Ni insertion into the Ar-Br bond.
